description: Issue #184: Tagged PDF / Structure Tree support

userStories.title: Parse StructTreeRoot and structure elements
userStories.description: Parse the /StructTreeRoot dictionary from the document catalog. Extract the structure tree hierarchy including structure elements (StructElem), their types (P, H1-H6, Table, Figure, Span, etc.), and MCID references. Store the structure tree in a queryable data structure.
acceptanceCriteria: StructTreeRoot is parsed from PDF catalog when present
Structure elements are extracted with type, MCID, and parent/child relationships
mcid_example.pdf structure tree is successfully parsed
figure_structure.pdf structure tree is successfully parsed
API: Page::structure_elements() returns structure elements for the page
userStories.notes: Key structure: /StructTreeRoot → /K (children) → /Type /StructElem entries. Each StructElem has /S (structure type), /K (content items or child elements), /P (parent). Content items reference MCIDs. The cross_validation entries for figure_structure, pdf_structure, hello_structure may just need updating since char extraction already works. Focus on mcid_example.pdf which still fails.

userStories.title: Map MCID tags to content for semantic reading order
userStories.description: Connect Marked Content IDs (MCIDs) from BMC/BDC/EMC operators in content streams to structure elements from the structure tree. This enables semantic reading order extraction — text ordered by document structure rather than content stream order.
acceptanceCriteria: MCID tags from content streams are associated with structure elements
Characters can be grouped by their parent structure element
Semantic reading order is available via API
mcid_example.pdf cross_validation upgraded from ignored to passing
userStories.notes: BMC/BDC operators already handled (skipped) by the interpreter. Need to: 1) Track current MCID during content stream interpretation, 2) Associate extracted chars with their MCID, 3) Use structure tree to determine reading order. The interpreter already has marked content handling from issue-167 fixes.
